title: Cross-origin redirects preserved custom authorization headers in fetchWithSsrFGuard
description: The fetchWithSsrFGuard function follows HTTP redirects manually but does not strip sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, Proxy-Authorization) when a redirect crosses origin boundaries. When the initial request to a trusted API returns a 3xx redirect to an attacker-controlled domain, the follow-up request carries the original Authorization and Cookie headers, leaking credentials to the external host.
